author: @PlanetJohnny
text:
I don't mind Subway employees asking what I want a couple of times, because it's a fast-paced environment wherein you have to keep track of a lot of shit, and sometimes they mishear something. Like, it's easy to mishear "Ranch" as "Frank's" (Frank's Red Hot), especially with machines going and other customers talking. I want to make sure they get my order right, so I don't mind them double checking.

What pisses me off at Subway are the other customers, especially if I'm going through the drive-thru. The drive-thru at a place with as many customization options as Subway should only be used by people who know the ordering process to a T, and already know exactly what they want and how they want it done.

author: @CaptainSpycrab
text:
Subway just so happens to be the only food place in walking distance of my university that's open 24/7, so I end up going there a lot.

I have a correspondingly high amount of Subway horror stories.

One time I went there and they were out of bread.  OUT OF BREAD.  YOU ARE A FUCKING SANDWICH SHOP.  BREAD IS YOUR LIFEBLOOD SWEAT AND TEARS.

Another time I went there at 2:30 AM only to discover a line that weaved through the restaurant before going out the goddamn door.

Yet another time I got my sandwich without incident, but then sat just at the cash register for literally an hour waiting for them to take my money.

Then there are the two homeless people that regularly sleep there.

Drunk people, high people, drunk servers, high servers... I could go on like this for hours.

author: @wherefancytakesme
text:
I agree with Arin on the spinach and having separate people for veggies and meat, but most restaurants do have a set number of how much of an ingredient goes on a thing so they'll only double it each time you ask for more. Annoying but that's just why it is that way. The biggest thing though is when a customer gets frustrated over the server asking them a very obvious question or repeating their order: The server HAS to do that. I've done service jobs and we have to ask them every question no matter how obvious so we can confirm/be extra sure we won't mess up what they want.
(Note: Yes I know Arin's just mostly being facetious for the bit)

author: @possumvomit7576
text:
Friend: what’s on your mind??

Me, internally: everything about Subway is-is like infuriating. Yeah, it’s like, the people in front of you take too long, and like there’s no drive-thru, and like, it-it’s just all … I don’t know, I’m over-exaggerating obviously, but Subway is like the land of inconveniences. ‘Cause then, it’s like I-urgh. I have to be, “okay” and then like they’re like, “what do you want?” and I’m like, “uhh … sweet onion chicken teriyaki footlong on flat bread.” and then they’re like, “what bread?” and it’s, ugh, “flat bread.” And they’re like, “oh, okay.” And then they, they fuckin’ take, and they’re like, “did you say footlong?” “Yes, I said footlong.” “You want cheese?” and it’s like, “you want cheese?” it’s like, “yeah can I get um … swiss cheese?” And they’re like, “what kind?” And it’s like, “fucking…” -yeah.. The Kind I Just Said. Ugh. And they’re like, “toasted?” and it’s like, “OF COURSE toas—you cant have the flatbread and not toast it! It’s-It’s like spongy, gross, starchy-ass fuckin’ bread. It’s made to be toasted! OF COURSE I want it toasted!” And then they fuckin’ toast it, and they start helping the people behind you, and then it sits in the toaster for like twenty seconds longer than it’s supposed to. And you’re like, “I JUST WANT LUNCH!” And then they fuckin’ take it out, and it’s like ANOTHER person, and then you have to fuckin’ get a read on THEIR personality. And it’s like, “God damn it.” And they’re like, “what do you want?” And it’s like, and I’m like, “uh spinach”, and then they just fucking DESTROY it with spinach just like an AVALANCHE of spinach and you’re like, “I want like vfhghFIVE other things! You can’t just fill it up with spinach and think that’s ALL it’s gonna be!” And then you fuckin’, and then you’re like, “onion” and they’re like, “okay” and they put like two onions on it, and it’s like, “MORE ONION PLEASE!” And then when you get like some ‘weird’ combination like fuckin’, “can I get mayonnaise, and also sweet onion sauce?” They like throw up their eyebrows a little bit like: “woah.” And you’re like, “Don’t fuckin’ JUDGE me! I’ll eat what I want! I can make my OWN sandwich!”

Me, aloud: nothing really haha

author: @akabiscuitwaffle
text:
Some subways actually do have drive throughs. 

It's just that, since it's optional, a additional investment cost to install, and not all locations can physically have one- most of the franchise owners opt to not get it. Which is compounded by the fact that all but 1 subway is a franchise. Only the ONE at the ground floor of the corporate office, is cooperate the owned. 
I've seen a grand total of 2 subways with drive throughs. One was a standalone building, and the other was a large gasstation that already had a wrap around drivethrough setup for a burgerking they had tacked onto it.

Frequently I will order and walk out with my food, and the line has not moved an inch when I drive away.

author: @supanerdreviews4056
text:
I work at a subway now and I can say while all of these are valid people behind the counter really have trouble when you try giving your entire order at once. We have questions we are supposed to ask 

“What can I get for you today?” 
Foot long on flat bread 

“What kind of sub?” 
Sweet onion chicken teriyaki 

“What kind of cheese?” And followed by “do you want it toasted?” Which is a question we are required to ask regardless of which bread you choose. 

The reason you’re getting less vegetables is because on a foot long the formula says you only get six of each aside from lettuce and spinach. Six pieces of onion. Six olives. Six jalapeños. You technically have to ask to get more but most employees do that automatically because they don’t wanna count out six of each veggie

And if we help the person behind you it’s because there’s 2 people in that store at any given time. Just two. At most 3. But if your sandwich is in the toaster for too long then it’s because the other person is busy in the back with prep or dishes because customers keep coming in when we finish someone’s order and prevents us from doing the tasks we are supposed to do to make sure we have the ingredients we need to make your sandwich in the first place

author: @xacmashe3852
text:
Arin is genuinely lucky his Subway doesnt have a drive through. Mine does.

Now imagine running around for 5 hours taking orders from a drive through, a phone, the computer and the people in the store. With a loud-ass toaster behind you, that's ALWAYS making noise. And then the store behind the line is hot, so your hands get a little sweaty, making your plastic gloves nigh impossible to put on due to them sticking to the moisture on your hands, causing them to rip CONSTANTLY. AND when you ring people up, they always bitch about their sandwich being 9 dollars after adding bacon and extra cheese. (A customer cursed out my manager and I wanted to punch his boomer ass in the fucking throat. My manager is a sweetheart and does her best for a Subway that has 3 employees including myself and her, and is notoriously old and bad. She didn't deserve that.)

Now imagine all this while being paid minimum wage.

I just had to put in perspective why your server asks you what bread you wanted. We're tired.
